data.title: record-test-status: full-suite parallel test execution causes fork exhaustion in resource-constrained environments
data.description:
### Incident Overview
When record-test-status.sh runs the full test suite (76+ staged files exceed the centrality threshold), parallel test execution exhausts the process fork limit. Tests like test-validate-phase-portability.sh spawn subprocesses that compound with the parallel runner, causing 'fork: retry: Resource temporarily unavailable' errors.

### Actual Behavior
The full suite records STATUS=failed due to resource exhaustion, not actual test failures. The failed_tests field is empty because the failure is at the OS level, not the test level. Subsequent runs with the same diff hash reuse the stale failed status.

### Expected Behavior
The test runner should respect resource constraints. Options: (1) detect fork exhaustion and retry with reduced parallelism, (2) cap MAX_PARALLEL based on available process slots, (3) serialize tests that spawn subprocesses.

### Workaround
Set MAX_PARALLEL=1 for affected tests (applied to test-validate-phase-portability.sh). Use record-test-status.sh --restart to clear stale status.
